danger_signals.visual_form: Third-person perspective view of opposing wizard character model on battlefield
Charnel-aligned creatures with death/decay visual theming
danger_signals.meaning: Life-drain attacks and decay-based offensive threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Intact opposing faction altar structures on map
danger_signals.meaning: Active enemy defensive position; desecration required for victory

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited soul resource count in inventory UI
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced creature summoning capacity; strategic disadvantage

danger_signals.visual_form: Flyer-class creatures dominating airspace above battlefield
danger_signals.meaning: Ranged units vulnerable; tactical disadvantage unless countered

safety_signals.visual_form: Wizard positioned near or at own altar structure
Erected manalith structure over mana fountain location
safety_signals.meaning: Monopolized mana regeneration point; resource advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Magical barrier visual effect around summoned creatures/units
safety_signals.meaning: Damage mitigation active; temporary defensive capability
Ongoing health recovery for units; sustained combat viability

safety_signals.visual_form: Larger groups of summoned creatures vs. opponent armies

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nature-aligned terrain features; healing spell visual signatures

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Undead creature models; life-drain spell effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corrupted/decay visual terrain; dark magical auras
Volcanic/geological spell effects; terrain manipulation abilities
Tornado/wind spell visual effects; aerial combat zones
Volcano/inferno visual manifestations; heat-based damage zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dynamic spell effects (tornadoes, volcanoes, barriers) rendering across battlefield in real-time
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Summoned creatures engage in rock-paper-scissors tactical positioning (melee vs. ranged vs. air)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wizard navigation toward unclaimed soul locations or defeated enemy creatures; manalith erection animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sac doctors extracting fallen opponent souls back to altars; manahoars channeling energy from manaliths

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desecration animation at enemy altar; sac doctor ritual casting at altar location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Victory condition progression; enemy altar structural integrity degradation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dimmed or depleted mana meter UI; reduced visual spell effect intensity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wizard forced to remain near altar or mana fountain for regeneration; reduced offensive capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Campaign cut-scenes rendered by game engine; Eldred flashback sequences showing tyrannical empire fall
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story progression through voiceovers with protagonist/Mithras dialogue narration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Summoned creatures display god-specific visual characteristics (undead for Charnel, nature effects for Persephone, elemental for others)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spells and creature abilities manifest according to chosen god's ethos (regeneration, decay, elements)

environmental_storytelling.example: Each player's altar serves as both defensive structure and resource-gathering nexus; desecration through sacrifice conveys political domination
Persephone's healing nature vs. Charnel's undead decay creates visual faction identity through creature models and spell effects
Scattered mana fountain locations across battlefield create resource hotspots; manalith erection marks territorial control
Rock-paper-scissors creature types (melee/ranged/air) establish tactical terrain relationships through visual positioning
Game-engine-rendered cut-scenes show Eldred's recounting of tyrannical empire fall to blind seer Mithras, establishing world political backstory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Aggressive spell-casting and creature summoning; active battlefield presence
Retreat toward altar or mana fountain; reduced spell availability; defensive positioning
Immobilized at enemy altar; executing desecration ritual; vulnerable to interruption
Ritual disruption; sacrifice fails; altar remains intact
Stationary near controlled manalith; transferring mana to summoner; support role
High damage output advantage; vulnerability to flyer counterattack
Effective counter to flyers; vulnerable to melee assault
Unreachable by melee units; vulnerable to ranged attacks; air-superiority role
Hidden from opponent sight; ambush capability; reveals on attack
Freezes opponent unit in place; prevents movement/retreat; tactical control

notes: Sacrifice uses a god-faction system (5 deities) as primary political/factional identity rather than traditional RTS factions. Visual communication relies heavily on spell effect manifestations, creature appearance theming, and altar-based control markers. The game emphasizes micromanagement and tactical unit positioning over base-building, making creature behavior and formation tells critical to world-state communication. Mana/soul resources are infinite/limited respectively, creating distinct resource-scarcity messaging. Third-person wizard perspective provides direct player avatar presence on battlefield, enhancing immersion in combat and narrative context.
